- Add optional expiryDate field to Ingredient class
- Add LocalDate expiry to add-i command parsing (ex/YYYY-MM-DD optional)
- Create Storage class for JSON save/load of recipes and ingredients
- Auto-load data on startup and auto-save on shutdown
- Add getRecipes() method to RecipeBook
- Add org.json dependency to build.gradle

commitResults.messageTitle: feat: Add ingredient expiry dates and JSON persistence
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add optional expiry date field to Ingredient (YYYY-MM-DD format)
- Implement Storage class for JSON serialization/deserialization
- Auto-load recipes and ingredients on startup
- Auto-save recipes and ingredients on shutdown
- Add org.json dependency to build.gradle
- Update Parser to support ex/YYYY-MM-DD parameter for add-i command
- Add getRecipes() method to RecipeBook for persistence access

commitResults.messageBody:
Fix the following bugs:
    1. Add-r accepts non-positive ingredient quantities, and cook can turn that into inventory
     creation. Parser.java:232 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L232),
     Parser.java:246 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L246),
     CookCommand.java:26 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/CookCommand.java#L26),
     DeleteIngredientCommand.java:66 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     DeleteIngredientCommand.java#L66), Inventory.java:64 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/
     sudocook/Inventory.java#L64). Repro: add-r Bad i/sugar -1 g s/{hack} t/1 c/1, add-i n/sugar q/1 u/
     g, cook 1 leaves sugar at 2.0 g and prints Removed -1.0 g of sugar.

    2. Cook without an index crashes the app instead of producing the documented error.
     Parser.java:292 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L292), Parser.java:297
     (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L297), SudoCook.java:51 (#/home/ubuntu/
     tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/SudoCook.java#L51). words[1] is read unconditionally and only
     NumberFormatException is caught, so cook throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

    3. Command parsing is prefix-based, so invalid commands can execute real actions.
     Parser.java:23 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L23), Parser.java:34 (#/
     home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L34), Parser.java:37 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/
     src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L37), Parser.java:51 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/
     seedu/sudocook/Parser.java#L51), Parser.java:81 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L81), Parser.java:103 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L103), Parser.java:128 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L128), Parser.java:184 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L184), Parser.java:256 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L256), Parser.java:292 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L292), Parser.java:303 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L303), Parser.java:307 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L307), Parser.java:315 (#/home/ubuntu/tp/src/main/java/seedu/sudocook/
     Parser.java#L315). Repro: cookbook 1 is parsed as cook 1 and executed; with a recipe present, I
     got Cooked RecipeSoup.
